title: Rome Old And Juli Eh

jokes.text: I feel like I'm back in wooden times!
jokes.setup: Homer examining wood paneling in the redone basement
jokes.punchline: His made-up historical era 'wooden times' as if wood had its own age
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer-ism - his childlike wonder expressed through completely made-up historical terminology. Classic character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Wow, the graphics are amazing. That ball almost seems real!
jokes.setup: Bart playing an old pinball machine
jokes.punchline: Marveling at 'graphics' of actual physical pinball as if it's video game technology
jokes.explanation: Smart generational joke about kids so used to video games they're amazed by physical reality.

jokes.text: What the...? But I thought bankruptcy was the cool law! The one that says 'Dude, don't worry about it. I got this.'
jokes.setup: Homer learning the new bankruptcy law requires paying creditors
jokes.punchline: His conception of bankruptcy law as a laid-back buddy saying 'I got this'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - personifying bankruptcy law as a casual friend. The 'Dude, don't worry about it' is perfectly quotable.

jokes.text: Permission to moan? / I'll allow it.
jokes.setup: Homer formally requesting permission for emotional response
jokes.punchline: Judge's matter-of-fact granting of moan permission
jokes.explanation: Perfect courtroom formality applied to basic human expression. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Of course, you idiot, 'cause I'm wishing for more money.
jokes.setup: Court noting Homer spends $1,000/month on wishing wells
jokes.punchline: Homer's circular logic - spending money to wish for money
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer financial illiteracy - the insult 'you idiot' while explaining his own idiotic logic.

jokes.text: Three subscriptions to Vanity Fair? / I've got three bathrooms, don't I?
jokes.setup: Officer questioning multiple magazine subscriptions
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical justification based on bathroom reading needs
jokes.explanation: Solid domestic logic joke. Homer's reasoning is actually semi-logical which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Shut up! It's an instructional website! Shut up!
jokes.setup: Questioned about $500/month to Totalpoker.com
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive overreaction with repeated 'Shut up!'
jokes.explanation: Basic defensive reaction comedy. The 'instructional website' excuse is predictable.

jokes.text: You promised me I could die here!
jokes.setup: Family removing Grampa from expensive nursing home
jokes.punchline: Grampa's morbid protest about his dying location promise
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect Grampa line - treating death location as a contract violation is wonderfully morbid.

jokes.text: I saw you doing a 500-piece jigsaw puzzle in there.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying nursing home expense
jokes.punchline: Citing jigsaw puzzle as evidence of excessive luxury
jokes.explanation: Homer's warped sense of luxury - jigsaw puzzles as extravagance is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: To save gas, I'm Flintstone-ing the car.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: Using 'Flintstone' as a verb for foot-powered driving
jokes.explanation: Clever verb creation from pop culture reference. 'Flintstone-ing' is instantly understandable and quotable.

jokes.text: So... very...
jokes.setup: Homer foot-powering the car
jokes.punchline: His exhausted reaction to the effort
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy payoff. Predictable exhaustion reaction to the Flintstone setup.

jokes.text: Keep Cool With Coolidge / Lilian Gish in Broken blossoms
jokes.setup: Grampa's bedroom posters being revealed
jokes.punchline: Extremely dated presidential slogan and silent film poster
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - Grampa's ancient cultural references establish his age comedically.

jokes.text: A.S.S. American Shipping Services Not affiliated with the human ass
jokes.setup: Company logo/sign visible
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate acronym requiring clarifying disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Basic acronym humor elevated by the overly specific disclaimer. Simpsons-style background gag.

jokes.text: Could a kid like me get these boxes, then use them for whatever he wanted? / Only if he fibbed about his name, age, and occupation on this automated 800 number.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about getting free boxes
jokes.punchline: Ned innocently providing detailed fraud instructions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ned characterization - his moral blind spot about helping kids commit minor fraud is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Nothing like un-tossing a leftover salad.
jokes.setup: Marge preparing to reuse salad ingredients
jokes.punchline: Innocent use of 'tossing salad' with unintended double meaning
jokes.explanation: Adult humor disguised in innocent domestic activity. Marge's obliviousness makes it work.

jokes.text: Hands off, you big blue Buttinski!
jokes.setup: Homer touching Grampa's medical samples
jokes.punchline: Grampa's old-fashioned insult combined with Marge's blue hair reference
jokes.explanation: Grampa's dated slang 'Buttinski' plus visual reference to Marge's hair. Mild but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Blood, bile, spinal fluid, blood, other, phlegm, miscellaneous ooze...
jokes.setup: Marge reading medical sample labels
jokes.punchline: Increasingly gross bodily fluids ending with vague 'miscellaneous ooze'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with 'miscellaneous ooze' as the perfect vague medical category.

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Grampa asking to sleep across Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate, emphatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy. The timing and emphasis carry this basic moment.

jokes.text: Maybe it's the bang-up job he did raising you. / He was a great dad. Every year he got so mad when Santa didn't bring me presents.
jokes.setup: Selma sarcastically praising Grampa's parenting
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious example that proves Selma's point about bad parenting
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic structure - Homer's 'defense' of his dad actually confirms he was terrible at parenting. Perfect character blindness.

jokes.text: I know what you're wondering. How come a single woman with so much to offer is alone on a Saturday night. / I assumed you were resting up for bingo tomorrow, like me.
jokes.setup: Selma's self-pitying assumption about others' thoughts
jokes.punchline: Grampa's innocent, age-appropriate assumption about bingo
jokes.explanation: Nice deflation of Selma's dramatic self-pity with Grampa's matter-of-fact elderly perspective.

jokes.text: My numbers don't get called much these days.
jokes.setup: Context of bingo discussion
jokes.punchline: Selma's double meaning about bingo numbers and romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre that works on both bingo and dating levels. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Wow, that's the first time anyone's ever put an adjective before calling me a thing.
jokes.setup: Grampa calling her a 'sweet young thing'
jokes.punchline: Selma's low expectations about being objectified
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware joke about objectification. Selma's low bar for compliments is both funny and slightly sad.

jokes.text: You know, I was voted best kisser in my P.O.W. camp.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment building
jokes.punchline: Grampa's dubious credential from prisoner of war camp
jokes.explanation: Dark humor meets romantic boasting. The absurd context of a P.O.W. camp kissing contest is perfectly Grampa.

jokes.text: Well, if he dies in my arms, I could meet a cute paramedic.
jokes.setup: Selma considering kissing elderly Grampa
jokes.punchline: Her morbid silver lining about meeting paramedics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - Selma finding a dating opportunity in Grampa's potential death. Perfectly cynical character moment.

jokes.text: All right, lips, man your kissing stations. This is not a drill.
jokes.setup: Grampa preparing to kiss Selma
jokes.punchline: Military-style commands to his own body parts
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Grampa line - treating romance like military operation. 'This is not a drill' perfectly escalates the absurdity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Ah! A bear is eating my father! / I'm Selma. / Ah! A talking bear is eating my father!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Selma and Grampa kissing
jokes.punchline: His horror escalating when the 'bear' talks back
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - Homer's horror compounds when the 'bear' responds. The talking bear is more terrifying than the regular bear.

jokes.text: I'm a little shaky on when and where, but I got my theories.
jokes.setup: Grampa asked if he knows who he was kissing
jokes.punchline: His confidence despite missing basic details
jokes.explanation: Good elderly confusion joke - Grampa's confidence despite fundamental uncertainty about basic facts.

jokes.text: Oh, why did I borrow that roof from him?
jokes.setup: Grampa asserting 'My roof, my rules'
jokes.punchline: Homer's regret about a past financial decision
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the financial crisis - even the roof is borrowed. Homer's poor planning comes back to haunt him.

jokes.text: Are you run by dogs or for dogs? Actually, don't answer. I like not knowing.
jokes.setup: Bart giving fake company name involving dogs
jokes.punchline: Rep's willful ignorance about suspicious details
jokes.explanation: Good corporate indifference joke - the rep actively choosing ignorance over investigation.

jokes.text: 'Build a fart.' I love it. / Fort. That's 'build a fort.' / That might work, too.
jokes.setup: Lisa correcting Bart's misreading
jokes.punchline: Bart's acceptance of both interpretations
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay elevated by Bart's willingness to consider both options. Mildly juvenile but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: At my age, and with your drawbacks, we can't afford to miss an opportunity.
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to convince Selma to continue dating
jokes.punchline: His brutally honest assessment of their mutual undesirability
jokes.explanation: Brilliant brutal honesty - Grampa's unromantic but practical approach to their mutual desperation. Perfectly economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Slow down, you hussy.
jokes.setup: Selma agreeing to share garlic bread
jokes.punchline: Grampa's old-fashioned moral judgment of her 'fast' behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa prudishness - treating sharing appetizers as scandalous. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Is she really going out with him? Is she really gonna take him home tonight?
jokes.setup: Homer watching Grampa and Selma
jokes.punchline: Parodying 'Mickey' by Toni Basil about his father's love life
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture parody but not particularly original or character-specific. Standard Simpsons musical reference.

jokes.text: It's like an old sea turtle dating a suitcase that fell out of a plane.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Grampa and Selma's relationship
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific metaphor comparing them to random objects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist metaphor - completely random but somehow captures the mismatch. The specificity of 'suitcase that fell out of a plane' is inspired.

jokes.text: Like how the parts of a pig nobody wants combine to make a yummy hot dog. / Oh, now you're dragging hot dogs into this. Real classy, Marge. Real classy.
jokes.setup: Marge's positive analogy about unwanted parts combining well
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant defense of hot dogs' reputation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's misplaced priorities defending hot dogs while missing Marge's romantic point.

jokes.text: We wrote that joke in the water. It wasn't all frolicking.
jokes.setup: Homer's garbage barge insult
jokes.punchline: Grampa revealing they collaborated on the comeback
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about joke construction - revealing the collaborative effort behind a spontaneous-seeming comeback.

jokes.text: Don't those two gargoyles know that love is for good-looking young people?
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing his father's relationship
jokes.punchline: His shallow, ageist philosophy about who deserves love
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's shallow worldview, though the sentiment is predictably awful.

jokes.text: Well, gee, Homer, you, uh, ain't exactly open-casket material yourself.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing others' looks
jokes.punchline: Barney's morbid assessment of Homer's attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant insult construction - 'open-casket material' as a beauty standard is darkly creative and memorable.

jokes.text: Look, if you want to break up your father and Selma, I have a plan. But it involves you. / Okay, but I'm not good at details. Or the big picture. I also show up late. And drunk.
jokes.setup: Patty proposing a scheme to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's comprehensive list of his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer self-awareness - cataloging his flaws while agreeing to help. The escalation from 'not good at details' to 'drunk' is excellent.

jokes.text: Lower the drawbridge! / What's the password? / I love my sister.
jokes.setup: Medieval fort roleplay with password request
jokes.punchline: Ned's wholesome family-oriented password
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders password - earnest family sentiment in a kids' game context. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I shall go, but I will return with an army of my brethren and together we shall take back what is ours and Hell will rain down upon you! / What if we're not here? / We will come two more times, and then you'll have to come to our customer center.
jokes.setup: Ned's dramatic medieval threat
jokes.punchline: Deflation to mundane corporate policy about delivery attempts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation/deflation - from biblical vengeance to shipping company policies. The structure is perfect.

jokes.text: It's near the airport, where the old Crown Books used to be.
jokes.setup: Ned giving directions to customer center
jokes.punchline: Overly specific landmark reference to defunct bookstore chain
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how people give directions using defunct businesses. Mild but relatable.

jokes.text: Hola, I am Esteban de la Sexface. That means 'Stephen of the Sexface.'
jokes.setup: Homer in disguise to fool Grampa
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake Spanish name with literal translation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist name construction. 'Sexface' is juvenile but the formal translation adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Okay, now let's prepare our mental images so we can kiss each other without barfing.
jokes.setup: Patty and Homer preparing to fake-kiss to fool Grampa
jokes.punchline: Her clinical approach to preventing nausea during the act
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - the unromantic preparation for fake romance. Patty's practical disgust is in character.

jokes.text: Dos Selmas? Ay-yi-yi!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing both Patty and Selma
jokes.punchline: His Spanish exclamation about 'two Selmas'
jokes.explanation: Basic bilingual pun playing on his Spanish disguise. Simple but economical.

jokes.text: I guess I always dreamed that my father would grow old alone.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his sabotage attempt
jokes.punchline: His honest admission of wanting his father to be miserable
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest character moment - Homer's selfish 'dream' for his father reveals his awful priorities.

jokes.text: A thousand, eh?
jokes.setup: Grampa saying no scheme could break them up
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it as a challenge with specific parameters
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer latching onto the specific number as if it's a goal to reach. Very economical.

jokes.text: Dad, don't throw rice, it makes the birds swell up! / Oh, Lisa, that's one of those rumors you get off the Internet.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about rice being dangerous to birds
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing it as internet misinformation (though it's actually true)
jokes.explanation: Decent role-reversal joke - Homer being skeptical of Lisa's actual knowledge while believing in bankruptcy as a 'cool law.'

jokes.text: Nothing solves everything forever like a wedding.
jokes.setup: Wedding scene conclusion
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive belief that marriage fixes all problems permanently
jokes.explanation: Good ironic closing line - Homer's optimistic delusion about marriage as a permanent solution to all problems.

tmdb_overview: Selma and Grampa embark on a May-December romance, and Bart and Lisa get into trouble with deliverymen over cardboard boxes.
